message:
feat(fcp-host): propagate asserted principal from batch-invoke HTTP header into preflight so header-forged principals fail closed

`batch_invoke_handler` previously dropped the incoming HTTP request
headers on the floor. It called `execute_batch_operation` once per
operation with only the app state and the operation itself, and
`execute_batch_operation` in turn called
`evaluate_live_preflight(&state, &request, None)` — passing `None` for
the principal override. That had two consequences:

1. The per-batch HTTP `X-FCP-Principal` (or equivalent — whatever
   `extract_principal_header` parses) was silently ignored. Single
   invoke requests already honor the header via live preflight; batch
   did not. That's a silent divergence in authZ surface between the two
   entry points.
2. Because the override was always `None`, there was no place for a
   mismatch between the token's holder and the asserted principal to
   surface as a deny. The token-holder check simply didn't fire for
   batch invocations, which is exactly the weakness an attacker trying
   to reuse a capability token with a different principal would exploit.

This commit threads the header through:

- `batch_invoke_handler` now takes `HeaderMap` (added as a second
  extractor) and derives `asserted_principal: Option<Arc<str>>` via
  `extract_principal_header(&headers)`. The value is added to the
  `batch_invoke_request` tracing span so auditors can correlate the
  asserted principal with the resulting deny/allow decisions.
- `execute_batch_operation` gains a `principal_override: Option<Arc<str>>`
  parameter and forwards it (via `as_deref()`) into
  `evaluate_live_preflight`. Every call site — including the chunked
  `join_all(ready...)` loop that drives parallel batch execution — now
  clones the `Arc<str>` into each operation's future, so the same
  asserted principal applies uniformly to every op in the batch.
- Tracing field `asserted_principal = asserted_principal.as_deref()`
  added to the top-of-handler debug event so the value is observable
  in logs regardless of outcome.

Test added (`batch_invoke_handler_rejects_principal_header_mismatch`):

- Spins up a batch with a single `test.echo` op whose capability token
  is held by one principal.
- Submits the batch with `X-FCP-Principal: user:other` in the headers.
- Asserts the response has `completed=0`, `failed=1`, a single
  `OperationResultStatus::Error` result, and — critically — that the
  error message includes `"request principal `user:other`"` so the
  deny fires in the preflight layer rather than after the operation
  starts executing.

The test uses the compiled `fcp-test-connector` binary and skips early
with a stderr note if the harness binary is not present, matching the
skip pattern used by other batch handler tests in this module.
